visa_types.name: Residence Permits
visa_types.overview:
If you intend to stay in Norway for more than 90 days, or if you are going to work while in Norway, you need to apply for a Residence Permit. 
If you apply for a residence permit, please note that the Embassy must have your passport together with the application in order to perform ID-control according to instructions from Ministry of Foreign
                                                            Affairs of Norway. This means that if you are applying from VFS Prishtina or from VFS Baku it will take at least one week before your passport is back in Prishtina or Baku.
If you are applying for a residence permit to Denmark, you are to follow the same process as if you were to apply for a residence permit to Norway. Please note that before you go to VFS Global in
                                                            Baku to hand in your application for a residence permit to Denmark, you will have to pay the case processing fee to the bank. For details about the application fee, please contact VFS Global.
                                                            You must pay the fee to the bank account of the Royal Norwegian Embassy in Ankara (https://www.norway.no/en/turkey/). Please make sure to print out a receipt of the payment and bring it with you to VFS Global when applying for
                                                            the permit. Here are the details for the bank account: 
Corporate name: Norveç Büyükelçılığı 
Account: 1361-1299382 TL 
IBAN: TR 84 0006 2001 3610 0001 2993 82 
Branch: TUNALIHILMI 
Swift code: TGBATRISXXX for Garanti Bank in Turkey

If you apply for a residence permit, please note that the Embassy must have your passport together with the application in order to perform ID-control according to instructions from Ministry of Foreign

If you are applying for a residence permit to Denmark, you are to follow the same process as if you were to apply for a residence permit to Norway. Please note that before you go to VFS Global in

visa_types.name: Business
visa_types.visa_fees:
When you apply for a visa or residence permit to Norway or the represented, you will pay the fee online when registering your application at the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/).

[Table:Tourist]
Attention Please : 
The following categories of applicants are exempted from the fee when submitting an application for Schengen Visa:
- Close relatives – spouses, children (including adopted), parents (including custodians), grandparents and grandchildren.
- Members of official delegations.
- Pupils, students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training.
- Persons with disabilities and persons accompanying them.
- Participants in international sport events and persons accompanying them in a professional capacity.
- Persons participating in scientific, cultural and artistic activities, including university and other exchange programs.
- Persons who have presented documents proving the necessity of their travel on humanitarian grounds, including to receive urgent medical treatment and the person accompanying such
                                                                            person, or to attend a funeral of a close relative, or to visit a seriously ill close relative.
- Representatives of civil society organisations when undertaking trips for the purposes of educational training, seminars, conferences, including in the framework of exchange programs.
- Pensioners.
- Children under 12 years of age and children under the age of 18 and dependent children under the age of 21 visiting Norway.
- Children under 12 years of age when travelling to Sweden and children under the age of 18 and dependent children under the age of 21 visiting Norway.
- Journalists and technical crew accompanying them in a professional capacity.
- Spouses, children and dependent ascendants of an EU/EEA national who is exercising free movement rights in Norway or the represented countries when visiting the person.
If you fall under one of the above-listed categories, the visa fee will be reimbursed by the Embassy to the credit card used for processing the payment. Please also note: 
- Except from visa fee, there will be 75 EUR VFS Global service fee will be paid in local currency.
Note: The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the Embassy of Norway while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport he/she has to pay additional 67.50 for resubmission fee and should be paid in local currency
- Courier is an optional service which will be charged 8.6 AZN plus fee for courier zone of your residence.
- VFS Global Service fee and additional services can only be paid in cash in AZN at the Visa Application Centers.
- Visa fees are only paid with credit card through the
Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/)
visa_types.processing_time: For processing time of visa applications from Azerbaijan to Norway you should plan for a processing time for up to 25 days (up to 10 days for case-processing time and up to 15 days for two way courier to Ankara)
visa_types.forms: The application form needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form, please visit the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/).
visa_types.overview:
Overview
A Schengen visa only allows foreign nationals to visit Norway and the other Schengen countries for up to 90 days. 
Please click on each of the tabs above for more information. For information about who is eligible to apply for a visa at the Visa Application Centre, please see Jurisdiction to Apply.
You can also find additional information about Visitor’s visa on the website of the Norwegian  Directorate of Immigration (UDI)  (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/)and
                                                                        the Royal Norwegian Embassy in Ankara (https://www.norway.no/en/turkey/).
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A Schengen visa only allows foreign nationals to visit Norway and the other Schengen countries for up to 90 days. 
Please click on each of the tabs above for more information. For information about who is eligible to apply for a visa at the Visa Application Centre, please see Jurisdiction to Apply.
You can also find additional information about Visitor’s visa on the website of the Norwegian  Directorate of Immigration (UDI)  (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/)and
                                                                        the Royal Norwegian Embassy in Ankara (https://www.norway.no/en/turkey/).

## Visa Fees
When you apply for a visa or residence permit to Norway or the represented, you will pay the fee online when registering your application at the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/).

[Table:Tourist]
Attention Please : 
The following categories of applicants are exempted from the fee when submitting an application for Schengen Visa:
- Close relatives – spouses, children (including adopted), parents (including custodians), grandparents and grandchildren.
- Members of official delegations.
- Pupils, students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training.
- Persons with disabilities and persons accompanying them.
- Participants in international sport events and persons accompanying them in a professional capacity.
- Persons participating in scientific, cultural and artistic activities, including university and other exchange programs.
- Persons who have presented documents proving the necessity of their travel on humanitarian grounds, including to receive urgent medical treatment and the person accompanying such
                                                                            person, or to attend a funeral of a close relative, or to visit a seriously ill close relative.
- Representatives of civil society organisations when undertaking trips for the purposes of educational training, seminars, conferences, including in the framework of exchange programs.
- Pensioners.
- Children under 12 years of age and children under the age of 18 and dependent children under the age of 21 visiting Norway.
- Children under 12 years of age when travelling to Sweden and children under the age of 18 and dependent children under the age of 21 visiting Norway.
- Journalists and technical crew accompanying them in a professional capacity.
- Spouses, children and dependent ascendants of an EU/EEA national who is exercising free movement rights in Norway or the represented countries when visiting the person.
If you fall under one of the above-listed categories, the visa fee will be reimbursed by the Embassy to the credit card used for processing the payment. Please also note: 
- Except from visa fee, there will be 75 EUR VFS Global service fee will be paid in local currency.
Note: The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the Embassy of Norway while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport he/she has to pay additional 67.50 for resubmission fee and should be paid in local currency
- Courier is an optional service which will be charged 8.6 AZN plus fee for courier zone of your residence.
- VFS Global Service fee and additional services can only be paid in cash in AZN at the Visa Application Centers.
- Visa fees are only paid with credit card through the
Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/)

## Documents Required
 Checklist

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (dated max 6 month old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be sized between 35 – 45 mm
- Full face taken straight forward 30 mm from point of the chin to top of hair
- Taken against a white background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
For further details with samples click here. 
Please note that the Visa application Centre may not accept photographs that do not meet these specifications. In case photos are not meeting the requirements mentioned above Submission
                                                                        Officer might ask to provide updated / recent photos. If they wish, applicants could get their proper sized biometric photographs from our Visa Application Center’s photobooth facility.

## Processing Time
For processing time of visa applications from Azerbaijan to Norway you should plan for a processing time for up to 25 days (up to 10 days for case-processing time and up to 15 days for two way courier to Ankara)

## Download Forms
The application form needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form, please visit the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/).

visa_types.overview:
Overview
 The purpose of the travel is to visit an EU/EEA citizen exercising free movement rights in Norway. Only spouses and children can apply, as well as parents who are dependent ascendants
                                                                        of the EU/EEA citizen exercising free movement rights in Norway. 
A Schengen visa only allows foreign nationals to visit Norway and the other Schengen countries for up to 90 days.
Please click on each of the tabs above for more information. You can also find additional information about Visitor’s visa on the website of the Norwegian  Directorate of Immigration (UDI)  (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/)and the Royal Norwegian Embassy in Ankara (https://www.norway.no/en/turkey/).

Overview
The purpose of the travel is to attend courses, internships, participation in Study/Exchange programs/Summer schools; participate in sports, training, cultural exchange programs, tours
                                                                        and participation in conferences, workshops, etc.
A Schengen visa only allows foreign nationals to visit Norway and the other Schengen countries for up to 90 days. 
Please click on each of the tabs above for more information. For information about who is eligible to apply for a visa at the Visa Application Centre, please see Jurisdiction to Apply.

                                                                        the Royal Norwegian Embassy in Ankara (https://www.norway.no/en/turkey/).

visa_types.name: Entry Visa for Waiting for a Decision in a Residence Case (‘’D-visa’’)
visa_types.documents_required:
The D-visa will only be granted if the Embassy has been provided with documents proving that the relationship is of a long lasting and serious character. In addition to the documentation
                                                                        required for an application for family immigration, you have to submit the documentation below if you apply for a D-visa for joining a spouse in Norway:
- A written statement from the reference about the relationship between the applicant and the reference, before and after the marriage took place. The reference should carefully explain
                                                                            how and where they met, and elaborate on the time they have spent together.
- The contact between the applicant and the reference should be documented (with a copy of the Norwegian spouse's passport showing entries to and exits from Turkey, and if applicable,
                                                                            a copy of the applicant's passport showing visits in Norway).
- If photos are submitted (optional) they have to be in A4-size.
The additional documents are not necessary if you are joining a spouse which has a residence permit as a skilled worker in Norway. 
You may not submit documents required for a C-visa when submitting an application for a D-visa.
Please note that if you apply for a D-visa separately, you have to submit all the documents required for a family immigration permit, even though you might have submitted the same documents
                                                                        earlier. 
If you apply for a D-visa at the same time as you apply for a family immigration permit, you only have to submit one set of the required documents for the family immigration permit. However,
                                                                        you will need to submit one separate cover letter for the D-visa application and one separate cover letter for the family immigration application.
visa_types.visa_fees:
The fee for applying for an entry visa for waiting for a decision in a residence case is 60 Euro. Applications for children under the age of 18 are free. 
For a complete list of fees, please see the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/).
                                                                        The fees are paid at the Application  Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/). 
Please note: 
- Except from visa fee, there will be 75 EUR VFS Global service fee will be paid in local currency.
Note: The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the Embassy of Norway  while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport he/she has to pay additional 67.50 EUR for resubmission fee and should be paid in local currency.
Courier is an optional service which will be charged 8.6 AZN plus fee for courier zone of your residence.
 VFS Service fee and additional services can only be paid in cash in AZN at the Visa Application Centers.
Visa fees are only paid with credit card through the Application  Portal Norway (https://selfservice.udi.no).
visa_types.processing_time:
The case-processing time for a D-visa application is 3 weeks (excluding day of submission at the Visa Application Centre). In some cases the processing time might be longer. If the applicant
                                                                        has not been interviewed by the Embassy, the processing time will be up to approximately 2 months.
visa_types.forms:
In order to apply for a D-visa, you need to register an application for a C-visa. To register an application online, please log on to the Application  Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/).
Form for granting Power of Attorney

 (https://www.udi.no/globalassets/global/skjemaer/power-of-attorney-e.pdf)
To download the Application Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your       computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your       mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to come to Norway through family immigration you must, as a rule, have been granted a residence permit before entering Norway. However, in exceptional cases when there is
                                                                        no doubt that all the criteria are met, you may enter Norway before you have been granted a residence permit. In such cases, you need an entry visa if you are subject to the visa
                                                                        requirement – a visa for applying for or waiting for a decision on an application for family immigration (D-visa). 
The Norwegian Embassy in Ankara will reject applications for a D-visa if the conditions are not met, or if the Embassy is in doubt about whether all the conditions are met. 
Please be aware that the police interviews the reference in most family immigration cases where the applicant is a Azeri citizen, and in all cases where the reference is under 25 years
                                                                        old. If the Embassy considers that there is a possibility that the police will make an interview of the reference in the family immigration case, the application for a D-visa will
                                                                        be rejected. 
If the reference has come to Norway on a family immigration permit, the application for a D-visa will normally not be granted.
In addition to spouses, also joint children who wish family immigration can apply. For more information about D-visas in family immigration cases, please see UDI Circular 2013-001 (http://www.udiregelverk.no/en/documents/udi-circulars/rs-2013-001/) and the web site of the Norwegian Embassy.
D-visas can also be given for the spouses/joint children of skilled workers with residence permits in Norway. Please see UDI  Circular 2013-004 (http://www.udiregelverk.no/en/documents/udi-circulars/rs-2013-004/). If you have questions about D-visas for waiting for a decision in a residence permit case with another purpose than family immigration,
                                                                        please contact the Embassy. 
In order to apply for a D-visa you must register an application for a C-visa in the Application Portal (https://www.udi.no/en/). The Embassy
                                                                        will change this into an application for a D-visa when they receive the application from the Visa Application Centre.
Only Azeri citizens can apply for a D-visa at the Visa Application Centre.
Please note:
D-visa is an entry visa which gives the applicant the right to wait in Norway for the decision in a residence permit case. It is not a residence permit, and the D-visa does not give the
                                                                        applicant right to work or access to any welfare services or health service benefits in Norway. If you have been granted a D-visa, the Embassy recommends that you purchase a private
                                                                        health insurance that will cover the whole period until your application for a residence permit has been decided.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to come to Norway through family immigration you must, as a rule, have been granted a residence permit before entering Norway. However, in exceptional cases when there is
                                                                        no doubt that all the criteria are met, you may enter Norway before you have been granted a residence permit. In such cases, you need an entry visa if you are subject to the visa
                                                                        requirement – a visa for applying for or waiting for a decision on an application for family immigration (D-visa). 
The Norwegian Embassy in Ankara will reject applications for a D-visa if the conditions are not met, or if the Embassy is in doubt about whether all the conditions are met. 
Please be aware that the police interviews the reference in most family immigration cases where the applicant is a Azeri citizen, and in all cases where the reference is under 25 years
                                                                        old. If the Embassy considers that there is a possibility that the police will make an interview of the reference in the family immigration case, the application for a D-visa will
                                                                        be rejected. 
If the reference has come to Norway on a family immigration permit, the application for a D-visa will normally not be granted.
In addition to spouses, also joint children who wish family immigration can apply. For more information about D-visas in family immigration cases, please see UDI Circular 2013-001 (http://www.udiregelverk.no/en/documents/udi-circulars/rs-2013-001/) and the web site of the Norwegian Embassy.
D-visas can also be given for the spouses/joint children of skilled workers with residence permits in Norway. Please see UDI  Circular 2013-004 (http://www.udiregelverk.no/en/documents/udi-circulars/rs-2013-004/). If you have questions about D-visas for waiting for a decision in a residence permit case with another purpose than family immigration,
                                                                        please contact the Embassy. 
In order to apply for a D-visa you must register an application for a C-visa in the Application Portal (https://www.udi.no/en/). The Embassy
                                                                        will change this into an application for a D-visa when they receive the application from the Visa Application Centre.
Only Azeri citizens can apply for a D-visa at the Visa Application Centre.
Please note:
D-visa is an entry visa which gives the applicant the right to wait in Norway for the decision in a residence permit case. It is not a residence permit, and the D-visa does not give the
                                                                        applicant right to work or access to any welfare services or health service benefits in Norway. If you have been granted a D-visa, the Embassy recommends that you purchase a private
                                                                        health insurance that will cover the whole period until your application for a residence permit has been decided.

## Visa Fees
 The fee for applying for an entry visa for waiting for a decision in a residence case is 60 Euro. Applications for children under the age of 18 are free. 
For a complete list of fees, please see the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/).
                                                                        The fees are paid at the Application  Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/). 
Please note: 
- Except from visa fee, there will be 75 EUR VFS Global service fee will be paid in local currency.
Note: The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the Embassy of Norway  while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport he/she has to pay additional 67.50 EUR for resubmission fee and should be paid in local currency.
Courier is an optional service which will be charged 8.6 AZN plus fee for courier zone of your residence.
 VFS Service fee and additional services can only be paid in cash in AZN at the Visa Application Centers.
Visa fees are only paid with credit card through the Application  Portal Norway (https://selfservice.udi.no).

## Documents Required
The D-visa will only be granted if the Embassy has been provided with documents proving that the relationship is of a long lasting and serious character. In addition to the documentation
                                                                        required for an application for family immigration, you have to submit the documentation below if you apply for a D-visa for joining a spouse in Norway:
- A written statement from the reference about the relationship between the applicant and the reference, before and after the marriage took place. The reference should carefully explain
                                                                            how and where they met, and elaborate on the time they have spent together.
- The contact between the applicant and the reference should be documented (with a copy of the Norwegian spouse's passport showing entries to and exits from Turkey, and if applicable,
                                                                            a copy of the applicant's passport showing visits in Norway).
- If photos are submitted (optional) they have to be in A4-size.
The additional documents are not necessary if you are joining a spouse which has a residence permit as a skilled worker in Norway. 
You may not submit documents required for a C-visa when submitting an application for a D-visa.
Please note that if you apply for a D-visa separately, you have to submit all the documents required for a family immigration permit, even though you might have submitted the same documents
                                                                        earlier. 
If you apply for a D-visa at the same time as you apply for a family immigration permit, you only have to submit one set of the required documents for the family immigration permit. However,
                                                                        you will need to submit one separate cover letter for the D-visa application and one separate cover letter for the family immigration application.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (dated max 6 month old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be sized between 35 – 45 mm
- Full face taken straight forward 30 mm from point of the chin to top of hair
- Taken against a white background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
For further details with samples click here. 
Please note that the Visa application Centre may not accept photographs that do not meet these specifications. In case photos are not meeting the requirements mentioned above Submission
                                                                        Officer might ask to provide updated / recent photos. If they wish, applicants could get their proper sized biometric photographs from our Visa Application Center’s photobooth facility.

## Processing Time
The case-processing time for a D-visa application is 3 weeks (excluding day of submission at the Visa Application Centre). In some cases the processing time might be longer. If the applicant
                                                                        has not been interviewed by the Embassy, the processing time will be up to approximately 2 months.

## Download Forms
In order to apply for a D-visa, you need to register an application for a C-visa. To register an application online, please log on to the Application  Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/).
Form for granting Power of Attorney

 (https://www.udi.no/globalassets/global/skjemaer/power-of-attorney-e.pdf)
To download the Application Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your       computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your       mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
You need a residence permit in order to work or take up residence for longer than 90 days. 
Please visit the web sites of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration  (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/) and the Royal Norwegian  Embassy in Ankara (https://www.norway.no/en/azerbaijan/) for more information about residence permits in Norway.

## Processing Time

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Family immigration is also called family reunification or forming a family. Those who apply for family immigration are usually the spouse, cohabitant or child of someone who lives in
                                                                        Norway. Other types of family members who may apply are parents who have children in Norway, those who are going to marry someone in Norway (fiancées/fiancés), foster children and
                                                                        full siblings. 
Only Azeri citizens can apply for a residence permit for family immigration purposes at the Visa Application Centre.
Please see the websites of the Norwegian Directorate for Immigration  (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) and the Norwegian Embassy (https://www.norway.no/en/turkey/) for more information about different family immigration permits.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Family immigration is also called family reunification or forming a family. Those who apply for family immigration are usually the spouse, cohabitant or child of someone who lives in
                                                                        Norway. Other types of family members who may apply are parents who have children in Norway, those who are going to marry someone in Norway (fiancées/fiancés), foster children and
                                                                        full siblings. 
Only Azeri citizens can apply for a residence permit for family immigration purposes at the Visa Application Centre.
Please see the websites of the Norwegian Directorate for Immigration  (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) and the Norwegian Embassy (https://www.norway.no/en/turkey/) for more information about different family immigration permits.

## Visa Fees
The fee for applying for a family immigration permit is 10.500 Norwegian Kroner. Applications for children under the age of 18 are free. 
For a complete list of fees, please see the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/).
                                                                        The fees are paid at the Application Portal Norway (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/). 
Please note: 
- Except from visa fee, there will be 75 EUR VFS Global service fee will be paid in local currency.
Note: The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the Embassy of Norway  while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport he/she has to pay additional 67.50 EUR for resubmission fee and should be paid in local currency.
Courier is an optional service which will be charged 8.6 AZN plus fee for courier zone of your residence.
 VFS Service fee can only be paid in cash in AZN at the Visa Application Centers. (will be added new courier service – two way courier service) 
Visa fees are only paid with credit card through the Application  Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/).

## Documents Required
For all checklists, please visit the website of the Norwegian Directorate for Immigration  (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explains-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/).
Please be aware that “Proof of the existence of family relations” should be submitted in all family immigration cases, even if it is not listed in the checklist from UDI.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (dated max 6 month old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be sized between 35 – 45 mm
- Full face taken straight forward 30 mm from point of the chin to top of hair
- Taken against a white background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
For further details with samples click here. 
Please note that the Visa application Centre may not accept photographs that do not meet these specifications. In case photos are not meeting the requirements mentioned above Submission
                                                                        Officer might ask to provide updated / recent photos. If they wish, applicants could get their proper sized biometric photographs from our Visa Application Center’s photobooth facility.

## Processing Time
Please click to see case processing  times. (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/case-processing-times/)

## Download Forms
The application form for a residence permit in Norway needs to be filled online. To register an application online, please log on to the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/).
For all checklists, please visit the website of the Norwegian Directorate for Immigration  (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explains-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/).
Form for granting Power of Attorney (https://www.udi.no/globalassets/global/skjemaer/power-of-attorney-e.pdf)
To download the Application Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your       computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your       mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.name: Tourist
visa_types.visa_fees:
When you apply for a visa or residence permit to Norway or the represented, you will pay the fee online when registering your application at the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/).

[Table:Tourist]
Attention Please : 
The following categories of applicants are exempted from the fee when submitting an application for Schengen Visa:
- Close relatives – spouses, children (including adopted), parents (including custodians), grandparents and grandchildren.
- Members of official delegations.
- Pupils, students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training.
- Persons with disabilities and persons accompanying them.
- Participants in international sport events and persons accompanying them in a professional capacity.
- Persons participating in scientific, cultural and artistic activities, including university and other exchange programs.
- Persons who have presented documents proving the necessity of their travel on humanitarian grounds, including to receive urgent medical treatment and the person accompanying such
                                                                            person, or to attend a funeral of a close relative, or to visit a seriously ill close relative.
- Representatives of civil society organisations when undertaking trips for the purposes of educational training, seminars, conferences, including in the framework of exchange programs.
- Pensioners.
- Children under 12 years of age and children under the age of 18 and dependent children under the age of 21 visiting Norway.
- Children under 12 years of age when travelling to Sweden and children under the age of 18 and dependent children under the age of 21 visiting Norway.
- Journalists and technical crew accompanying them in a professional capacity.
- Spouses, children and dependent ascendants of an EU/EEA national who is exercising free movement rights in Norway or the represented countries when visiting the person.
If you fall under one of the above-listed categories, the visa fee will be reimbursed by the Embassy to the credit card used for processing the payment. Please also note: 
- Except from visa fee, there will be 75 EUR VFS Global service fee will be paid in local currency.
Note: The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the Embassy of Norway while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport he/she has to pay additional 67.50 for resubmission fee and should be paid in local currency
- Courier is an optional service which will be charged 8.6 AZN plus fee for courier zone of your residence.
- VFS Global Service fee and additional services can only be paid in cash in AZN at the Visa Application Centers.
- Visa fees are only paid with credit card through the
Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/)
visa_types.processing_time: For processing time of visa applications from Azerbaijan to Norway you should plan for a processing time for up to 25 days (up to 10 days for case-processing time and up to 15 days for two way courier to Ankara)
visa_types.forms: The application form needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form, please visit the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/).
visa_types.overview:
Overview
The purpose of the visit is to travel to Norway, or the represented countries, as a tourist, either by taking a trip organised by a tour operator, or through individual booking of tickets
                                                                        and accommodation.
 A Schengen visa only allows foreign nationals to visit Norway and the other Schengen countries for up to 90 days. 
Please click on each of the tabs above for more information. For information about who is eligible to apply for a visa at the Visa Application Centre, please see Jurisdiction to Apply.
You can also find additional information about Visitor’s visa on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI)  (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/)and
                                                                        the Royal Norwegian Embassy in Ankara (https://www.norway.no/en/turkey/).
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The purpose of the visit is to travel to Norway, or the represented countries, as a tourist, either by taking a trip organised by a tour operator, or through individual booking of tickets
                                                                        and accommodation.
 A Schengen visa only allows foreign nationals to visit Norway and the other Schengen countries for up to 90 days. 
Please click on each of the tabs above for more information. For information about who is eligible to apply for a visa at the Visa Application Centre, please see Jurisdiction to Apply.
You can also find additional information about Visitor’s visa on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI)  (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/)and
                                                                        the Royal Norwegian Embassy in Ankara (https://www.norway.no/en/turkey/).

## Visa Fees
When you apply for a visa or residence permit to Norway or the represented, you will pay the fee online when registering your application at the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/).

[Table:Tourist]
Attention Please : 
The following categories of applicants are exempted from the fee when submitting an application for Schengen Visa:
- Close relatives – spouses, children (including adopted), parents (including custodians), grandparents and grandchildren.
- Members of official delegations.
- Pupils, students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training.
- Persons with disabilities and persons accompanying them.
- Participants in international sport events and persons accompanying them in a professional capacity.
- Persons participating in scientific, cultural and artistic activities, including university and other exchange programs.
- Persons who have presented documents proving the necessity of their travel on humanitarian grounds, including to receive urgent medical treatment and the person accompanying such
                                                                            person, or to attend a funeral of a close relative, or to visit a seriously ill close relative.
- Representatives of civil society organisations when undertaking trips for the purposes of educational training, seminars, conferences, including in the framework of exchange programs.
- Pensioners.
- Children under 12 years of age and children under the age of 18 and dependent children under the age of 21 visiting Norway.
- Children under 12 years of age when travelling to Sweden and children under the age of 18 and dependent children under the age of 21 visiting Norway.
- Journalists and technical crew accompanying them in a professional capacity.
- Spouses, children and dependent ascendants of an EU/EEA national who is exercising free movement rights in Norway or the represented countries when visiting the person.
If you fall under one of the above-listed categories, the visa fee will be reimbursed by the Embassy to the credit card used for processing the payment. Please also note: 
- Except from visa fee, there will be 75 EUR VFS Global service fee will be paid in local currency.
Note: The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the Embassy of Norway while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport he/she has to pay additional 67.50 for resubmission fee and should be paid in local currency
- Courier is an optional service which will be charged 8.6 AZN plus fee for courier zone of your residence.
- VFS Global Service fee and additional services can only be paid in cash in AZN at the Visa Application Centers.
- Visa fees are only paid with credit card through the
Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/)

## Documents Required
 Checklist

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (dated max 6 month old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be sized between 35 – 45 mm
- Full face taken straight forward 30 mm from point of the chin to top of hair
- Taken against a white background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
For further details with samples click here. 
Please note that the Visa application Centre may not accept photographs that do not meet these specifications. In case photos are not meeting the requirements mentioned above Submission
                                                                        Officer might ask to provide updated / recent photos. If they wish, applicants could get their proper sized biometric photographs from our Visa Application Center’s photobooth facility.

## Processing Time
For processing time of visa applications from Azerbaijan to Norway you should plan for a processing time for up to 25 days (up to 10 days for case-processing time and up to 15 days for two way courier to Ankara)

## Download Forms
The application form needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form, please visit the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/).

visa_types.name: D-VISA
visa_types.overview: For information about D-Visas, please go to Entry Visit For Waiting For A Decision In A Residence Case (“D-Visa”).
visa_types.full_text: For information about D-Visas, please go to Entry Visit For Waiting For A Decision In A Residence Case (“D-Visa”).

visa_types.name: Visiting Family and Friends
visa_types.visa_fees:
When you apply for a visa or residence permit to Norway or the represented, you will pay the fee online when registering your application at the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/).

[Table:Tourist]
Attention Please : 
The following categories of applicants are exempted from the fee when submitting an application for Schengen Visa:
- Close relatives – spouses, children (including adopted), parents (including custodians), grandparents and grandchildren.
- Members of official delegations.
- Pupils, students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training.
- Persons with disabilities and persons accompanying them.
- Participants in international sport events and persons accompanying them in a professional capacity.
- Persons participating in scientific, cultural and artistic activities, including university and other exchange programs.
- Persons who have presented documents proving the necessity of their travel on humanitarian grounds, including to receive urgent medical treatment and the person accompanying such
                                                                            person, or to attend a funeral of a close relative, or to visit a seriously ill close relative.
- Representatives of civil society organisations when undertaking trips for the purposes of educational training, seminars, conferences, including in the framework of exchange programs.
- Pensioners.
- Children under 12 years of age and children under the age of 18 and dependent children under the age of 21 visiting Norway.
- Children under 12 years of age when travelling to Sweden and children under the age of 18 and dependent children under the age of 21 visiting Norway.
- Journalists and technical crew accompanying them in a professional capacity.
- Spouses, children and dependent ascendants of an EU/EEA national who is exercising free movement rights in Norway or the represented countries when visiting the person.
If you fall under one of the above-listed categories, the visa fee will be reimbursed by the Embassy to the credit card used for processing the payment. Please also note: 
- Except from visa fee, there will be 75 EUR VFS Global service fee will be paid in local currency.
Note: The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the Embassy of Norway while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport he/she has to pay additional 67.50 for resubmission fee and should be paid in local currency
- Courier is an optional service which will be charged 8.6 AZN plus fee for courier zone of your residence.
- VFS Global Service fee and additional services can only be paid in cash in AZN at the Visa Application Centers.
- Visa fees are only paid with credit card through the
Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/)
visa_types.processing_time: For processing time of visa applications from Azerbaijan to Norway you should plan for a processing time for up to 25 days (up to 10 days for case-processing time and up to 15 days for two way courier to Ankara)
visa_types.forms: The application form needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form, please visit the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/).
visa_types.overview:
Overview
The purpose of the travel is to visit family or friends in Norway. The person you are going to visit will have to provide an invitation (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/invitation-for-a-visitors-visa-/) and in most cases a financial guarantee (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/guarantee-for-applicants-visiting-on-a-visitors-visa-/).
A Schengen visa only allows foreign nationals to visit Norway and the other Schengen countries for up to 90 days. 
Please click on each of the tabs above for more information. For information about who is eligible to apply for a visa at the Visa Application Centre, please see Jurisdiction to Apply.
You can also find additional information about Visitor’s visa on the website of the Norwegian  Directorate of Immigration (UDI)  (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/)and
                                                                        the Royal Norwegian Embassy in Ankara (https://www.norway.no/en/turkey/) .

## Overview
The purpose of the travel is to visit family or friends in Norway. The person you are going to visit will have to provide an invitation (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/invitation-for-a-visitors-visa-/) and in most cases a financial guarantee (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/guarantee-for-applicants-visiting-on-a-visitors-visa-/).
A Schengen visa only allows foreign nationals to visit Norway and the other Schengen countries for up to 90 days. 
Please click on each of the tabs above for more information. For information about who is eligible to apply for a visa at the Visa Application Centre, please see Jurisdiction to Apply.
You can also find additional information about Visitor’s visa on the website of the Norwegian  Directorate of Immigration (UDI)  (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/)and
                                                                        the Royal Norwegian Embassy in Ankara (https://www.norway.no/en/turkey/) .

## Visa Fees
When you apply for a visa or residence permit to Norway or the represented, you will pay the fee online when registering your application at the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/).

[Table:Tourist]
Attention Please : 
The following categories of applicants are exempted from the fee when submitting an application for Schengen Visa:
- Close relatives – spouses, children (including adopted), parents (including custodians), grandparents and grandchildren.
- Members of official delegations.
- Pupils, students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training.
- Persons with disabilities and persons accompanying them.
- Participants in international sport events and persons accompanying them in a professional capacity.
- Persons participating in scientific, cultural and artistic activities, including university and other exchange programs.
- Persons who have presented documents proving the necessity of their travel on humanitarian grounds, including to receive urgent medical treatment and the person accompanying such
                                                                            person, or to attend a funeral of a close relative, or to visit a seriously ill close relative.
- Representatives of civil society organisations when undertaking trips for the purposes of educational training, seminars, conferences, including in the framework of exchange programs.
- Pensioners.
- Children under 12 years of age and children under the age of 18 and dependent children under the age of 21 visiting Norway.
- Children under 12 years of age when travelling to Sweden and children under the age of 18 and dependent children under the age of 21 visiting Norway.
- Journalists and technical crew accompanying them in a professional capacity.
- Spouses, children and dependent ascendants of an EU/EEA national who is exercising free movement rights in Norway or the represented countries when visiting the person.
If you fall under one of the above-listed categories, the visa fee will be reimbursed by the Embassy to the credit card used for processing the payment. Please also note: 
- Except from visa fee, there will be 75 EUR VFS Global service fee will be paid in local currency.
Note: The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the Embassy of Norway while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport he/she has to pay additional 67.50 for resubmission fee and should be paid in local currency
- Courier is an optional service which will be charged 8.6 AZN plus fee for courier zone of your residence.
- VFS Global Service fee and additional services can only be paid in cash in AZN at the Visa Application Centers.
- Visa fees are only paid with credit card through the
Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/)

## Documents Required
 Checklist

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (dated max 6 month old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be sized between 35 – 45 mm
- Full face taken straight forward 30 mm from point of the chin to top of hair
- Taken against a white background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
For further details with samples click here. 
Please note that the Visa application Centre may not accept photographs that do not meet these specifications. In case photos are not meeting the requirements mentioned above Submission
                                                                        Officer might ask to provide updated / recent photos. If they wish, applicants could get their proper sized biometric photographs from our Visa Application Center’s photobooth facility.

## Processing Time
For processing time of visa applications from Azerbaijan to Norway you should plan for a processing time for up to 25 days (up to 10 days for case-processing time and up to 15 days for two way courier to Ankara)

## Download Forms
The application form needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form, please visit the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/).

visa_types.name: Short Term Visas (Type C)
visa_types.overview:
If you plan to visit Norway for less than 90 days, you can apply for a visitor's visa. With this visa, you can visit all Schengen countries. 
New regional system for immigration cases As from 03 August 2018, applications for C visa or residence permit will be processed by the Norwegian embassy in Ankara – Republic
                                                            of Turkey. However, applications will still be received through VFS Global in Azerbaijan after this date. 
Apply and collect as before!
You will still be able to submit the application and collect the decision letter from the same visa application center as before. Whereas the application procedures remain the same, please take note
                                                            of the following changes when applying to go to Norway from 03 August 2018: 
Extended processing time:
For processing time of visa applications from Azerbaijan to Norway you should plan for a processing time for up to 25 days (up to 10 days for case-processing time and up to 15 days for two way courier
                                                            to Ankara).
- Introduction of a courier fee
As before, there will be a visa application fee, and a service fee charged by VFS Global. In addition there will be courier fee to cover transportation
                                                                of your documents between the visa application center and Ankara – Republic of Turkey.
See our ‘Visa fees at a glance’-section for further information.
